2605.29141.title: Toward User Preference Alignment in LLM Recommendation via Explicit Context Feedback
2605.29141.paper_summary: Traditional recommender systems (RecSys) primarily infer user preferences from implicit signals (such as clicks, watches, and purchases), often neglecting the rich explicit contextual feedback users provide through verbal text, like comments and reviews. This explicit context feedback captures the nuanced reasons behind user decisions regarding their preferences. In addition, it offers critical heterogeneous information for user preference alignment and more explainable recommendations. Overlooking such signals can lead to misaligned user preferences and further reinforce filter bubbles, as algorithms fail to understand the "semantic context" behind user choices. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) present new opportunities to harness user-generated content for more accurate and diverse recommendations, yet current LLM-based recommendations still focus on using item meta-data and underutilize this resource. In this paper, we advocate for prioritizing explicit context feedback in the next generation of LLM-based RecSys. We review the evolution of recommendation paradigms, highlight the value of context-rich feedback, call for new benchmarks and metrics, and introduce frameworks for integrating explicit user signals into scalable LLM-driven RecSys. Centering on user-preference modeling, we aim to foster more personalized, transparent, and explainable RecSys online platforms.
2605.29141.paper_summary_zh: 傳統的推薦系統（RecSys）主要從隱性信號（如點擊、觀看和購買）推斷用戶偏好，往往忽略了用戶通過文字（如評論和評價）提供的豐富明確上下文反饋。這種明確的上下文反饋捕捉了用戶在其偏好決策背後的微妙原因。此外，它還提供了用於用戶偏好對齊和更具可解釋性的推薦的重要異質信息。忽視這些信號可能導致用戶偏好的不對齊，並進一步加強過濾泡沫，因為算法未能理解用戶選擇背後的“語義上下文”。最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展為利用用戶生成內容提供了新的機會，以實現更準確和多樣化的推薦，但目前基於LLM的推薦仍然專注於使用項目元數據，並未充分利用這一資源。在本文中，我們主張在下一代基於LLM的RecSys中優先考慮明確的上下文反饋。我們回顧了推薦範式的演變，強調了豐富上下文反饋的價值，呼籲新的基準和指標，並介紹了將明確用戶信號整合到可擴展的LLM驅動RecSys中的框架。以用戶偏好建模為中心，我們旨在促進更個性化、透明和可解釋的RecSys在線平台。
2605.29141.author: Weizhi Zhang et.al.
2605.29141.authors: Weizhi Zhang, Wooseong Yang, Yuxin Cui, Zhaohui Guo, Hins Hu, Liangwei Yang, Henry Peng Zou, Qifei Wang, Hanqing Zeng, Jiayi Liu, Yinglong Xia, Philip S. Yu
